title: CIS Controls v8 — Full Program Build from Zero
summary: Built the organization's compliance program from nothing — full CIS Controls v8 implementation across all 18 domains, crosswalked to the HIPAA Security Rule, audited 100% pass, and architected for HITRUST CSF readiness.
problem: There was no compliance program. No documented controls, no crosswalk to HIPAA, no audit posture. The organization was operating in a HIPAA-regulated environment without any framework mapping the regulatory obligations to actual technical and administrative controls.
approach: Mapped every CIS Controls v8 safeguard to the corresponding HIPAA Security Rule citation, producing a single crosswalk document leadership and auditors could both read
Implemented controls domain-by-domain over six months, sequencing by risk reduction per dollar — inventory and access management before more advanced detection
Deployed CIS-CAT Pro for continuous configuration assessment so posture drift surfaces between audits, not at them
Built a monthly board-level posture dashboard tracking control maturity, gaps, and remediation timelines in business terms
Engaged third-party penetration testing against the matured program — passed on first attempt
Architected the entire program for forward compatibility with HITRUST CSF certification, even though formal cert is a later milestone
lessons: Compliance programs fail when they are run as documentation projects instead of engineering projects. Every control needs to map to a real technical or procedural artifact you can point to in an audit — not a paragraph in a binder. The crosswalk between CIS v8 and the HIPAA Security Rule is the single most useful document I built; auditors and leadership both read it differently and both find what they need. Build the crosswalk first, then build the controls.
